title: Trump declares US-Iran peace deal 'now complete,' as Iran confirms 'immediate end' to the war
subhead: The initial announcement from mediator Pakistan was quickly confirmed by Washington and Tehran, and an official signing was scheduled for June 19 in Switzerland. Trump said Sunday the Strait of Hormuz, a vital route for global oil and natural gas supplies, would open after the ceremony.

title: Regulating military AI, a challenge debated in Geneva alongside the G7
Universal basic income, the utopian idea resurging in Silicon Valley
US imposes its domination on part of Latin America
subhead: Most Latin American countries, even non-aligned ones, have folded to the Trump administration's demands. Washington has not hesitated to use threats of sanctions or visa withdrawals to push its interests, with little regard for the rule of law.

title: Baudouin, the former king of the Belgians who was willing to do anything to stop abortion
subhead: Personal diaries and archives shed new light on the ethical and spiritual dilemmas faced by the former monarch in the years before his death, just as Belgium was preparing to legalize abortion.

title: Is it ever a good idea to... wear a beret?
subhead: In fashion, as in life, it is important to know the lines you should not cross. Or else, to do so deliberately.

title: Nicolas Kssis-Martov, sports historian: 'Football has always embodied the political stakes of the left'
subhead: In his latest book, the journalist delivers an impassioned work centered on key figures and historical moments, rooting football in a progressive vision of hope.

When Darwin comes to town: How the urban jungle disrupts animals' natural selection
subhead: Whether it's more aggressive chickadees, snails with lighter coloring or pigeons with anti-pollution plumage, urban wildlife is trying to adapt to city life. Is it behavioral flexibility or true genetic evolution? For the past 10 years, researchers have been investigating.

title: Marjane Satrapi's comic strips for Le Monde, 20 years ago
subhead: Best known for her masterpiece 'Persepolis,' the French-Iranian graphic novelist and filmmaker, who died on June 4, published comic strips in issues of Le Monde's magazine.

title: France prepares for another 'very intense and widespread' heat event, possibly over 40°C
subhead: The southern half of France is set to be hit first this weekend. Starting Tuesday, the heat is expected to worsen and move northward. Temperatures could reach or exceed 40°C, although some uncertainty remains.

title: 'While the G7 is indisputably obsolescent, nothing seems capable of replacing it'
